description: Reusable output class templates referenced by methods. Each concrete template is a named code-list of statistics: the canonical declaration of which constituents an output class produces. Templates are organised SKOS-style into abstract families that group templates sharing intent and partly sharing constituents; concrete templates belong to a family via 'broader'. Per D27/D32, the template is the single source of truth for what statistics appear in the output.

_authoring_notes.method_side_references: A method's output_specification.results entry references a concrete template by name (e.g. { "output_type": "fit_statistics_linear" }). Methods MUST NOT reference abstract templates. Method entries should NOT redeclare statistics — those are on the template. Method entries may add: name (only when disambiguating multiple slots of the same template type), label (method-specific override of the template's label), indexed_by (which input-slot values index the rows of this method's output table; combinations via ':' represent interactions), configurations (method-specific configuration overrides).
_authoring_notes.templates_are_code_lists: Each concrete template is a named code-list of statistics. Templates declare what their members ARE (label, description, the statistics list). They do not declare shape metadata (cardinality, dimensions) about how outputs that use them are indexed — that is the method's responsibility via indexed_by, because the same template can be used in differently-shaped outputs across methods.
_authoring_notes.template_vs_method: Templates declare what is structurally possible (the constituents that CAN appear). Instance data declares what was actually computed (which constituents are populated for a particular run).
_authoring_notes.skos_organization: Concrete templates are organised under abstract family parents via 'broader'. Abstract families group templates that share intent and partly share constituents. Methods reference concrete children, never abstract parents. Current family structure:

fit_statistics.label: Fit statistics
fit_statistics.description: Family of overall model goodness-of-fit summary tables.

fit_statistics_basic.label: Basic fit statistics
fit_statistics_basic.description: Basic model fit indices common to most likelihood-based models.

fit_statistics_concordance.label: Concordance-based fit statistics
fit_statistics_concordance.description: Fit indices including the concordance statistic (logistic regression, Cox PH).

type3_tests_f.label: Type III F-tests
type3_tests_f.description: Type III F-tests with sums-of-squares decomposition (linear models).

type3_tests_mixed.label: Type III F-tests (mixed/multivariate)
type3_tests_mixed.description: Type III F-tests for mixed and multivariate models (no SS/MS exposure).

type3_tests_chi_squared.label: Type III chi-squared tests
type3_tests_chi_squared.description: Type III Wald or LR chi-squared tests (logistic regression, Cox PH).

test_result.label: Test result
test_result.description: Family of single-row test result tables.

t_test_result.label: T-test result
t_test_result.description: Single t-test result (one-sample, paired, or two-sample).

exact_test_result.label: Exact test result
exact_test_result.description: Exact test result (Fisher exact) — p-value only.

homogeneity_test.label: Homogeneity test
homogeneity_test.description: Breslow-Day test for homogeneity of odds ratios across strata.

parameter_estimates.label: Parameter estimates
parameter_estimates.description: Family of per-coefficient model parameter tables.

parameter_estimates_glm.label: GLM parameter estimates
parameter_estimates_glm.description: GLM coefficients with z-based inference; includes odds_ratio for logistic.

parameter_estimates_cox.label: Cox PH parameter estimates
parameter_estimates_cox.description: Cox PH coefficients with z-based inference; includes hazard_ratio.

covariance_parameters.label: Covariance parameters
covariance_parameters.description: Estimated covariance/variance components of the random-effects structure.

contrasts.label: Contrasts
contrasts.description: Family of contrast tables — linear combinations of model coefficients with inference.

contrasts_t.label: T-based contrasts
contrasts_t.description: Contrasts from linear models with t-based inference.

contrasts_z.label: Z-based contrasts
contrasts_z.description: Contrasts from GLM or Cox PH with z-based inference.

association_measures.label: Association measures
association_measures.description: Family of effect-size / association measure tables with inference.

odds_ratio_measures.label: Odds ratio measures
odds_ratio_measures.description: Odds ratio with confidence interval (CMH common OR or per-stratum, Fisher OR).

marginal_estimates.label: Marginal estimates
marginal_estimates.description: Family of model-derived estimates at specific factor levels — adjusted means and predictions from a fitted model, distinct from raw coefficient estimates.

ls_means.label: Least-squares means
ls_means.description: Least-squares means (adjusted marginal means) per factor level or level combination.

point_estimate.label: Point estimate
point_estimate.description: Estimated mean (or paired-difference mean) with inference.

exponentiated_estimates.label: Exponentiated estimates
exponentiated_estimates.description: Family of coefficients exponentiated from a transformed scale (log, logit) back to the natural scale of multiplicative effects.

odds_ratio_estimates.label: Odds ratio estimates
odds_ratio_estimates.description: Odds ratios with confidence intervals (exponentiated logistic coefficients).

hazard_ratio_estimates.label: Hazard ratio estimates
hazard_ratio_estimates.description: Hazard ratios with confidence intervals (exponentiated Cox coefficients).

omnibus_tests.label: Omnibus tests
omnibus_tests.description: Family of overall-model significance tests indexed by test type (Wilks, Pillai, likelihood ratio, Wald, score, etc.), distinct from per-term tests.

multivariate_tests.label: Multivariate tests
multivariate_tests.description: Multivariate test statistics for overall hypothesis (Wilks, Pillai, Hotelling-Lawley, Roy).

global_tests.label: Global tests
global_tests.description: Overall model significance tests (likelihood ratio, Wald, score).

survival_outputs.label: Survival outputs
survival_outputs.description: Family of outputs from time-to-event analyses — life tables, median survival, event counts, landmark estimates.

survival_table.label: Survival table
survival_table.description: Life table with survival estimates at each event time.

event_summary.label: Event summary
event_summary.description: Summary counts of events and censoring per group.

landmark_estimates.label: Landmark estimates
landmark_estimates.description: Survival probability at specific landmark times.

group_summaries.label: Group summaries
group_summaries.description: Family of descriptive summaries broken down by group — proportions, frequency tables, quartile estimates. No model fit involved.

proportion_estimate.label: Proportion estimate
proportion_estimate.description: Group-wise proportion with optional confidence interval.

frequency_table.label: Frequency table
frequency_table.description: Counts of response categories per group.

cumulative_frequency_table.label: Cumulative frequency table
cumulative_frequency_table.description: Cumulative counts and percentages of response categories per group.

quartile_estimates.label: Quartile estimates
quartile_estimates.description: Q1 and Q3 quartile estimates per group.
